summary: Iteration 6 (Razorpay webhook + GDPR/DPDP wallet export + App.jsx refactor) — ALL backend & frontend verification points PASS. Created /app/backend/tests/test_webhook_export.py (11 tests, 100% pass): invalid signature → 400, missing header → 400, payment.captured → 200{ok:true,applied:'payment.captured'}, duplicate event → 200{duplicate:true}, payment.failed → applied, refund.created → applied, /api/user/export sans auth → 401, JSON export validates exported_at/exported_for/spec_compliance.dpdp_2023/spec_compliance.gdpr + all 7 collections (vouchers, circle_members, app_membership, payments, notifications, referrals, support_history), CSV starts with '# Perk Orbit · Personal Data Export' + has '## vouchers' / '## payments' sections, format=xml → 422 (Query pattern), and a created TEST_BrandExport voucher round-trips into export.collections.vouchers. Frontend Playwright: signup → PIN(1234x2) → walkthrough → Home → ProfileMenu(avatar) → Privacy Control screen renders with 'Export my wallet' card containing data-testid=export-json + export-csv. page.expect_download() captured a real browser download with filename perk-orbit-export-2026-06-17T11-39-41.json. App.jsx refactor (4 sheets + format helpers extracted) is non-breaking — entire first-launch flow completes without errors. Build health check at /app/scripts/health_check.py: 12/12 HEALTHY.
critical_code_review_comments: Pre-existing: App.jsx is still large (refactor extracted only 4 sheets + format helpers). Further extraction of screen components (HomeScreen, MyCouponsScreen, etc.) would improve maintainability — but not blocking this iteration.

webhook_export.py uses event['id'] as idempotency key with a fallback to payment.entity.id. Recommend adding a unique index on db.webhook_events.event_id to make the dedupe race-safe under concurrent retries (current code does a read-then-insert which has a small TOCTOU window). Optional hardening.

Export endpoint reads ALL of a user's data into memory and serializes in one shot. Fine for current scale but for very heavy users (e.g. thousands of notifications), streaming JSON line-by-line or a StreamingResponse generator would be more memory-friendly. Not blocking.
test_credentials: Used fresh signup per run (e2e-{ts}@perkorbit.app / test1234, PIN 1234). Webhook HMAC computed from RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET in /app/backend/.env (= perkorbit_test_webhook_secret_2026).
seed_data_creation: Created throwaway TEST_export_{ts}@perkorbit.app cloud account + 1 TEST_BrandExport voucher in test_export_contains_user_voucher (uses TEST_ prefix per cleanup guidelines).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 is fully green. Webhook signature must be HMAC-SHA256 of the EXACT raw body bytes (no re-serialization) using RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Use json.dumps(body, separators=(',', ':')) and post via requests with data=body_bytes so signature matches. Idempotency keyed on event['id'] in db.webhook_events. /api/user/export auth is via cookie (preferred) or Bearer token from /api/auth/login (both work — the test fixture uses Session() so cookies attach automatically). For Playwright signup flow: the 'Sign up' switch is a text link 'Create an account →', not a tab. PIN entry digits are still button:has-text(d) (no per-key data-testid). data-testid=profile-avatar is top-RIGHT (panic-lock-btn moved to top-LEFT in iter 5 — no overlay collision). Privacy Control buttons export-json + export-csv trigger a real browser download initiated by api.exportData() (blob → URL.createObjectURL → anchor.click). No bugs found.
